tickets.title: Liveness heartbeat + stall watchdog
tickets.scope: Persist a heartbeat on every poll and every posted review (last_poll_at, last_review_at, monotonically-advancing poll counter) to a durable location (state DB or heartbeat file). Add a stall watchdog - in-process timer that exits 75 (launchd respawn) on no-forward-progress in N seconds, and/or a sibling liveness probe mirroring sentinel-liveness that restarts the watcher when the heartbeat is stale despite the process being up. Catches the alive-but-not-progressing stall the inode-only detector (watcher.mjs:1735) missed. Tests: a simulated wedged poll loop trips the watchdog and exits 75; a healthy watcher does not.

tickets.title: Broaden the DB-connection health canary
tickets.scope: The existing inode-orphan check (watcher.mjs:1735-1752) only catches the DB file being replaced. Add a periodic write-canary (a cheap UPDATE/PRAGMA user_version round-trip or heartbeat-row write+read-back) so a connection silently failing writes for ANY reason triggers the same exit-75 restart. Tests: a connection whose writes silently no-op trips the canary and exits 75; existing inode-orphan detection still works.

tickets.title: Outage-aware attempt-budget accounting
tickets.scope: Classify a review/remediation attempt failure as transient when it coincides with a detected system outage - deploy wedge (main-catchup freezeClass), quota outage (quota-exhaustion.mjs), or broker/gh unavailability - versus a genuine review failure. A transient/outage failure must NOT set review_status='failed' terminally, NOT increment the terminal review_attempts budget, and be re-queued for a fresh attempt once the outage clears (pause-and-resume not fail-and-exhaust). Surface outage.active, reviews_paused, attempts_not_charged in health output. Tests: an attempt failing during a simulated outage does not increment review_attempts or set terminal failed and is re-queued on recovery; a genuine (non-outage) failure still counts and terminalizes.

tickets.title: Reopen-aware review-state reset
tickets.scope: When a PR's pr_state transitions from closed/merged back to open (reopened), reset a terminal review_status (failed/failed-orphan) so it becomes eligible for a fresh first-pass review, instead of staying permanently ineligible on a stale row (the exact state hand-cleared in the incident). Keep audit history (don't blindly delete); reset the eligibility fields. Tests: a reopened PR with a prior terminal failed row is re-reviewed; a genuinely-closed PR is not.
